methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Machina Labs is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $80M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 9 private robotics peers and 1 public comparable, selected for sector alignment and stage proximity.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $80M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $152M. The peer-median method identifies four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B Series C)—with a median valuation of $1.6B and a range of $1.0B to $1.8B.

The two methods produce substantially divergent signals. Triangulated across both approaches, the analysis supports a valuation range of $129M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $888M. The wide dispersion reflects fundamental uncertainty around whether Machina Labs' capital efficiency and stage positioning align with the lower end implied by capital-raised multiples or the peer median reflective of private robotics market valuations. Resolution of this gap depends on traction metrics, unit economics, and market positioning relative to the peer set.
